note: Control IDs and short titles only — ISO copyright applies to full control text.

controls.title: Policies for information security
controls.description: Management-approved information security policies must be defined, published, communicated to staff and relevant external parties, and reviewed regularly

controls.title: Information security roles and responsibilities
controls.description: Security responsibilities must be clearly defined and allocated to specific individuals according to organisational needs

controls.title: Segregation of duties
controls.description: Conflicting duties and areas of responsibility must be separated to reduce opportunities for unauthorised or unintentional modification of assets

controls.title: Management responsibilities
controls.description: Management must require all personnel to apply information security in accordance with established policies and procedures

controls.title: Contact with authorities
controls.description: Appropriate contacts with relevant authorities must be established and maintained to support incident handling and regulatory obligations

controls.title: Contact with special interest groups
controls.description: Relationships with security forums and professional associations must be maintained to stay informed about evolving threats and best practices

controls.title: Threat intelligence
controls.description: Information about security threats must be collected and analysed to produce actionable intelligence supporting defensive measures

controls.title: Information security in project management
controls.description: Security considerations must be integrated into project management activities regardless of the type or scope of the project

controls.title: Inventory of information and other associated assets
controls.description: A complete inventory of information and associated assets must be developed, maintained, and assigned to responsible owners

controls.title: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
controls.description: Rules for acceptable use and handling of information and associated assets must be documented and enforced

controls.title: Return of assets
controls.description: Personnel and external parties must return all organisational assets in their possession upon termination of employment or contract

controls.title: Classification of information
controls.description: Information must be classified based on confidentiality, integrity, availability, and relevant stakeholder requirements

controls.title: Labelling of information
controls.description: Procedures for labelling information must be implemented in accordance with the organisation's classification scheme

controls.title: Information transfer
controls.description: Rules, procedures, and agreements must govern the transfer of information within the organisation and with external parties

controls.title: Access control
controls.description: Rules controlling physical and logical access to information and associated assets must be defined and applied based on business and security needs

controls.title: Identity management
controls.description: The full lifecycle of identities used to access information must be managed including creation, modification, and removal

controls.title: Authentication information
controls.description: Allocation and management of authentication information such as passwords and tokens must follow a controlled process

controls.title: Access rights
controls.description: Access rights to information and assets must be provisioned, reviewed, modified, and revoked in line with the access control policy

controls.title: Information security in supplier relationships
controls.description: Processes must be defined to manage information security risks associated with the use of suppliers and their products or services

controls.title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
controls.description: Relevant security requirements must be established and agreed with each supplier based on the type of supplier relationship

controls.title: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
controls.description: Risks associated with the ICT products and services supply chain must be identified and managed through appropriate controls

controls.title: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services
controls.description: Changes to supplier services must be regularly monitored, reviewed, evaluated, and managed to maintain agreed security levels

controls.title: Information security for use of cloud services
controls.description: Processes for acquisition, use, management, and exit from cloud services must be established to meet security requirements

controls.title: Information security incident management planning and preparation
controls.description: The organisation must plan and prepare for managing information security incidents through defined processes, roles, and procedures

controls.title: Assessment and decision on information security events
controls.description: Security events must be assessed and a decision made on whether they should be categorised as security incidents

controls.title: Response to information security incidents
controls.description: Information security incidents must be responded to according to documented procedures by appropriately trained personnel

controls.title: Learning from information security incidents
controls.description: Knowledge gained from incidents must be used to strengthen and improve security controls and reduce likelihood of recurrence

controls.title: Collection of evidence
controls.description: Procedures must be defined for the identification, collection, acquisition, and preservation of evidence related to security events

controls.title: Information security during disruption
controls.description: Plans must be in place to maintain information security at an appropriate level during disruptions to normal operations

controls.title: ICT readiness for business continuity
controls.description: ICT readiness must be planned, implemented, maintained, and tested based on business continuity and ICT continuity requirements

controls.title: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
controls.description: Legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements relevant to information security must be identified, documented, and kept up to date

controls.title: Intellectual property rights
controls.description: Appropriate procedures must be implemented to protect intellectual property rights of the organisation and third parties

controls.title: Protection of records
controls.description: Records must be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorised access, and unauthorised release in accordance with requirements

controls.title: Privacy and protection of PII
controls.description: Requirements for preserving privacy and protecting personally identifiable information must be identified and met through appropriate controls

controls.title: Independent review of information security
controls.description: The approach to managing information security must be reviewed independently at planned intervals and after significant changes

controls.title: Compliance with policies, rules and standards for information security
controls.description: Compliance with security policies, rules, and standards must be regularly reviewed to verify ongoing adherence

controls.title: Documented operating procedures
controls.description: Operating procedures for information processing facilities must be documented and made available to personnel who need them

controls.title: Screening
controls.description: Background verification checks on candidates must be carried out prior to joining and proportionate to business and security risks

controls.title: Terms and conditions of employment
controls.description: Employment contracts must state the responsibilities of personnel and the organisation regarding information security

controls.title: Information security awareness, education and training
controls.description: Personnel and relevant interested parties must receive appropriate security awareness, education, and training relevant to their roles

controls.title: Disciplinary process
controls.description: A formal disciplinary process must be communicated and used to take action against personnel who violate security policy

controls.title: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
controls.description: Security responsibilities that remain valid after termination or change of employment must be defined, enforced, and communicated

controls.title: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements
controls.description: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting protection needs must be identified, documented, reviewed, and signed by relevant parties

controls.title: Remote working
controls.description: Security measures must be implemented when personnel work remotely to protect information accessed, processed, or stored outside premises

controls.title: Information security event reporting
controls.description: A mechanism must be provided for personnel to report observed or suspected security events through appropriate channels in a timely manner

controls.title: Physical security perimeters
controls.description: Security perimeters must be defined and used to protect areas containing information and other associated assets

controls.title: Physical entry
controls.description: Secure areas must be protected by appropriate entry controls and access points to prevent unauthorised physical access

controls.title: Securing offices, rooms and facilities
controls.description: Physical security must be designed and applied to offices, rooms, and facilities housing sensitive information or assets

controls.title: Physical security monitoring
controls.description: Premises must be continuously monitored for unauthorised physical access using appropriate detection and surveillance systems

controls.title: Protecting against physical and environmental threats
controls.description: Protection must be designed and implemented against physical and environmental threats such as natural disasters and intentional attacks

controls.title: Working in secure areas
controls.description: Security measures for working in secure areas must be designed and implemented to protect activities conducted within them

controls.title: Clear desk and clear screen
controls.description: Clear desk rules for papers and removable media and clear screen rules for processing facilities must be defined and enforced

controls.title: Equipment siting and protection
controls.description: Equipment must be sited securely and protected against environmental threats, hazards, and opportunities for unauthorised access

controls.title: Security of assets off-premises
controls.description: Assets located off-site must be protected taking into account the different risks faced compared to those on-premises

controls.title: Storage media
controls.description: Storage media must be managed through its lifecycle of acquisition, use, transportation, and disposal in line with classification and handling requirements

controls.title: Supporting utilities
controls.description: Information processing facilities must be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities

controls.title: Cabling security
controls.description: Cables carrying power, data, or supporting information services must be protected from interception, interference, or damage

controls.title: Equipment maintenance
controls.description: Equipment must be maintained correctly to ensure availability, integrity, and confidentiality of information it processes or stores

controls.title: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
controls.description: Items of equipment containing storage media must be verified to ensure sensitive data and licensed software are removed before disposal or re-use

controls.title: User endpoint devices
controls.description: Information stored on, processed by, or accessible via user endpoint devices must be protected through appropriate technical and policy controls

controls.title: Privileged access rights
controls.description: The allocation and use of privileged access rights must be restricted and managed through a formal authorisation process

controls.title: Information access restriction
controls.description: Access to information and associated assets must be restricted in accordance with the established access control policy

controls.title: Access to source code
controls.description: Read and write access to source code, development tools, and software libraries must be appropriately managed and restricted

controls.title: Secure authentication
controls.description: Secure authentication technologies and procedures must be implemented based on access restrictions and the information access control policy

controls.title: Capacity management
controls.description: The use of resources must be monitored and adjusted in line with current and expected capacity requirements to maintain performance

controls.title: Protection against malware
controls.description: Protection against malware must be implemented and supported by appropriate user awareness and detection technology

controls.title: Management of technical vulnerabilities
controls.description: Information about technical vulnerabilities must be obtained and evaluated, and appropriate measures must be taken to address associated risks

controls.title: Configuration management
controls.description: Configurations of hardware, software, services, and networks must be established, documented, implemented, monitored, and reviewed

controls.title: Information deletion
controls.description: Information stored in systems, devices, or other storage media must be deleted when no longer required to reduce exposure of sensitive data

controls.title: Data masking
controls.description: Data masking must be used in accordance with the access control policy and legal requirements to limit exposure of sensitive information

controls.title: Data leakage prevention
controls.description: Measures must be applied to systems and networks that process sensitive information to detect and prevent unauthorised disclosure or exfiltration

controls.title: Information backup
controls.description: Backup copies of information, software, and systems must be maintained and regularly tested in accordance with an agreed backup policy

controls.title: Redundancy of information processing facilities
controls.description: Information processing facilities must be implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements

controls.title: Logging
controls.description: Logs recording activities, exceptions, faults, and security events must be produced, stored, protected, and analysed

controls.title: Monitoring activities
controls.description: Networks, systems, and applications must be monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential incidents

controls.title: Clock synchronization
controls.description: Clocks of systems used for information processing must be synchronised to approved time sources to support accurate logging and forensics

controls.title: Use of privileged utility programs
controls.description: The use of utility programs capable of overriding system and application controls must be restricted and tightly controlled

controls.title: Installation of software on operational systems
controls.description: Procedures and measures must be implemented to securely manage the installation of software on operational systems

controls.title: Networks security
controls.description: Networks and network devices must be secured, managed, and controlled to protect information in systems and applications

controls.title: Security of network services
controls.description: Security mechanisms, service levels, and service requirements for network services must be identified, implemented, and monitored

controls.title: Segregation of networks
controls.description: Groups of information services, users, and information systems must be segregated within the organisation's networks

controls.title: Web filtering
controls.description: Access to external websites must be managed to reduce exposure to malicious content and inappropriate material

controls.title: Use of cryptography
controls.description: Rules for the effective use of cryptography including key management must be defined and implemented across the organisation

controls.title: Secure development life cycle
controls.description: Rules for the secure development of software and systems must be established and applied throughout the development lifecycle

controls.title: Application security requirements
controls.description: Information security requirements must be identified, specified, and approved when developing or acquiring applications

controls.title: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
controls.description: Principles for engineering secure systems must be established, documented, maintained, and applied to development activities

controls.title: Secure coding
controls.description: Secure coding principles must be applied to software development to reduce the number of potential security vulnerabilities

controls.title: Security testing in development and acceptance
controls.description: Security testing processes must be defined and implemented during the development lifecycle and prior to system acceptance

controls.title: Outsourced development
controls.description: Outsourced system development activities must be directed, monitored, and reviewed to ensure security requirements are met

controls.title: Separation of development, test and production environments
controls.description: Development, testing, and production environments must be separated and secured to protect production data and systems

controls.title: Change management
controls.description: Changes to information processing facilities and information systems must be subject to formal change management procedures

controls.title: Test information
controls.description: Test information must be appropriately selected, protected, and managed to avoid exposure of sensitive production data

controls.title: Protection of information systems during audit testing
controls.description: Audit tests and similar assurance activities on operational systems must be planned and agreed to minimise disruption
